reasoning: Both audits failed at source download stage (makepkg --nobuild), preventing examination of critical build/install scripts and source code where security risks would manifest. Initial PKGBUILD review was clean but insufficient for full assessment.
coverage_issues: Complete source code unreviewed due to download failure
Critical files like build scripts, install hooks, and Rust source code with network/filesystem access never analyzed
false_negatives: Potential risks in build()/package() functions, cargo dependencies, or mps.service unreviewed
Possible malicious code in uncompiled source or build-time hooks
learnings: Tool must fail gracefully when source download fails – attempt manual download or flag dependency issues
Prioritize files that execute during makepkg (PKGBUILD functions, .install) over incidental files
Improve error handling for network-dependent operations in audit pipeline
Add checksum verification fallback when upstream sources are unavailable
